name: geopandas
description: Python library for working with geospatial vector data including shapefiles, GeoJSON, and GeoPackage files. Use when working with geographic data for spatial analysis, geometric operations, coordinate transformations, spatial joins, overlay operations, choropleth mapping, or any task involving reading/writing/analyzing vector geographic data. Supports PostGIS databases, interactive maps, and integration with matplotlib/folium/cartopy. Use for tasks like buffer analysis, spatial joins between datasets, dissolving boundaries, clipping data, calculating areas/distances, reprojecting coordinate systems, creating maps, or converting between spatial file formats.
qualityReason: Specific library, clear data use case
capabilities: Read and write spatial data in Shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, and Parquet formats
Perform spatial joins, overlays, and dissolve operations on vector geometries
Transform coordinates between CRS (EPSG codes, WKT, PROJ strings)
Create buffer, simplify, centroid, and affine transformations on geometries
Generate choropleth maps and interactive maps with folium integration
Query PostGIS databases and cloud storage (S3, Azure, HTTP)
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown files with Python code examples. All 228 static analyzer findings are false positives: the analyzer misinterpreted Python API calls (gdf.to_crs, gdf.geometry.buffer) as shell commands, EPSG geospatial codes as cryptographic algorithms, and attribute access operations as system reconnaissance. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: GeoPandas extends pandas for geospatial operations. It enables reading shapefiles, GeoJSON, and PostGIS data. Perform spatial joins, buffer analysis, coordinate transformations, and create choropleth maps with matplotlib integration.
limitations: Only handles vector data (points, lines, polygons), not raster or grid data
Does not include geocoding, routing, or network analysis capabilities
Requires additional packages for interactive maps (folium) or advanced projections (cartopy)

useCases.title: Spatial Data Analysis
useCases.description: Analyze geographic patterns in datasets by joining spatial features, calculating areas, and identifying intersections.

useCases.title: Urban Development Mapping
useCases.description: Load shapefiles, clip to study areas, dissolve boundaries, and create publication-ready choropleth maps.

useCases.title: Environmental Impact Studies
useCases.description: Combine multiple spatial datasets, perform overlay analysis, and track changes over time with coordinate transformations.

promptTemplates.title: Load and explore data
promptTemplates.prompt: Read a shapefile and show its CRS, geometry types, and basic statistics

promptTemplates.title: Perform spatial join
promptTemplates.prompt: Join point locations to polygon boundaries using a spatial join with the intersects predicate

promptTemplates.title: Create buffer analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 500-meter buffer around all features and calculate the total buffered area

promptTemplates.title: Build interactive map
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an interactive folium map with a choropleth layer colored by population values

bestPractices: Always check CRS before spatial operations and reproject to a projected CRS for accurate area and distance measurements
Use spatial indexes and bbox filtering during file reads to improve performance with large datasets
Validate geometries with .is_valid before complex operations and repair invalid features using .buffer(0)
antiPatterns: Do not calculate areas or distances using geographic CRS (EPSG:4326) - results will be meaningless in degrees
Do not ignore geometry validity - invalid polygons can cause unexpected results in spatial joins
Do not use Shapefile format for production data - it has 2GB file size limit and poor datetime support

faq.question: What formats can GeoPandas read?
faq.answer: GeoPandas reads Shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, KML, Parquet, Feather, and PostGIS via SQL queries. Cloud storage via fsspec is also supported.

faq.question: How do I convert between coordinate systems?
faq.answer: Use gdf.to_crs(new_crs) to transform coordinates. Accepts EPSG codes (3857), authority strings (EPSG:4326), WKT, or PROJ strings.

faq.question: What is the difference between spatial join types?
faq.answer: sjoin uses predicate-based matching (intersects, within, contains). sjoin_nearest finds the closest feature. Use how='inner' or 'left' to control output.

faq.question: Why are my area calculations wrong?
faq.answer: Geographic CRS like EPSG:4326 measure in degrees, not meters. Reproject to a projected CRS (UTM, Albers) before calculating areas.

faq.question: Can I create interactive web maps?
faq.answer: Yes, use .explore() method with folium to create interactive maps with tooltips, legends, and multiple layers that can be saved as HTML.

faq.question: How do I filter data during read?
faq.answer: Use bbox parameter for bounding box filtering, rows for row limits, columns to select attributes, and where for SQL-style filtering.
